name: MISRA C++: Directives Addressed With Evidence
description: Directives (process/design guidelines, whose compliance depends on project context) are addressed with documented evidence.
rationale: MISRA: a Directive's compliance cannot be fully defined by the guideline alone; the project must show evidence it has been addressed.

examples.fails: the required compliance artifact/step is absent

source: MISRA Compliance:2020 — Achieving compliance with MISRA Coding Guidelines, applied to MISRA C++:2023. https://misra.org.uk/
